body_txt: Our protagonist is in a Chain Gang prison and has violated one of the Small-Town Tyrant sheriff's rules. So they send him to the Punishment Box (more commonly, "The Box.") The Box is commonly a small metal shed in the middle of the compound (in some cases too small to even sit down in), exposed to sun. They put you in and you are tortured by sweating it out in 120-plus degree heat, basically frying you alive. Unless it's a cold night, in which case you have to keep from freezing to death. Various forms of this have been an unfortunate Truth in Television (see The Other Wiki for a few examples), which is probably where the trope originates from. A very, very downplayed gentle version is sometimes used in training small pets; for example, ferrets can be trained out of biting by putting them in their carrying cage or a similar confinement with no toys or attention for one minute at a time immediately after the bite (any longer and they forget why they're in there). Although the box could be made of cardboard, especially in comedic examples, this is not to be confused with Cardboard Prison . See also Iron Maiden , which is even less beneficial to your health, and Closet Punishment .
